title: A novel endogenous retention-index for minimizing retention-time variations in metabolomic analysis with reversed-phase ultrahigh-performance liquid-chromatography and mass spectrometry.
author_string: Chen, Qinsheng, Lu, Qinwei, Zhang, Lianglong, Zhang, Chenhan, Zhang, Jingxian et al.
abstract: Consistent retention time (t<sub>R</sub>) of metabolites is vital for identification in metabolomic analysis with ultrahigh-performance liquid-chromatography (UPLC). To minimize inter-experimental t<sub>R</sub> variations from the reversed-phase UPLC-MS, we developed an endogenous retention-index (endoRI) using in-sample straight-chain acylcarnitines with different chain-length (L<sub>C</sub>, C0-C26) without additives. The endoRI-corrections reduced the t<sub>R</sub> variations caused by the combined changes of mobile phases, gradients, flow-rates, elution time, columns and temperature from up to 5.1 min-0.2 min for most metabolites in a model metabolome consisting of 91 metabolites and multiple biological matrices including human serum, plasma, fecal, urine, A549 cells and rabbit liver extracts. The endoRI-corrections also reduced the inter-batch and inter-platform t<sub>R</sub> variations from 1.5 min to 0.15 min for 95 % of detected features in the above biological samples. We further established a quantitative model between t<sub>R</sub> and L<sub>C</sub> for predicting t<sub>R</sub> values of acylcarnitines when absent in samples. This makes it possible to compare metabolites' t<sub>R</sub> from different t<sub>R</sub> databases and the UPLC-based metabolomic data from different batches.
